errors.title: Least Upper Bound Property Implies Existence of Suprema
errors.message: A standard statement/formal field appears to contain predicate notation. House style reserves canonical predicate names for predicate-reading blocks; standard quantified, negated, contrapositive, and statement fields should use standard mathematical notation.
errors.excerpt: \[ \begin{aligned} &\text{Let }S\text{ be an ordered set with the least upper bound property.}\\ &\forall A\subseteq S\; \bigl[ A\ne\varnothing \land \exists u\in S\;\forall x\in A\;(x\le u) \Longrightarrow \exists s\in S\;(s=\sup_S A) \bigr]. \end{aligned} \]

errors.title: Least Upper Bound Property
errors.message: A standard statement/formal field appears to contain predicate notation. House style reserves canonical predicate names for predicate-reading blocks; standard quantified, negated, contrapositive, and statement fields should use standard mathematical notation.
errors.excerpt: \[ \begin{aligned} &\text{Let }(S,\le)\text{ be an ordered set}.\\ &S\text{ has the Least Upper Bound Property}\\ &\Longleftrightarrow \forall A\subseteq S\; \Bigl[ \bigl(A\neq\varnothing \land \exists u\in S\;\forall x\in A\;(x\le u)\bigr) \rightarrow \exists s\in S\;\operatorname{Supremum}(s,A) \Bigr]. \end{aligned} \]

errors.title: Absolute Maximum and Absolute Minimum
errors.message: Extracted theorem/definition box appears to contain more than one primary statement. Extraction was preserved, but the source should be split into separate boxes and re-extracted.
errors.excerpt: Let $A \subseteq \mathbb{R}$ and let $f \colon A \to \mathbb{R}$. A point $x^{*} \in A$ is an absolute maximum of $f$ on $A$ if and only if $f(x^{*}) \ge f(x)$ for every $x \in A$. A point $x_{*} \in A$ is an absolute minimum of $f$ on $A$ if and only if $f(x_{*}) \le f(x)$ for every $x \in A$.

errors.title: Algebra of Uniform Continuity on a Bounded Domain
errors.message: Extracted theorem/definition box appears to contain more than one primary statement. Extraction was preserved, but the source should be split into separate boxes and re-extracted.
errors.excerpt: Let $A \subseteq \mathbb{R}$ be bounded, and let $f,g:A \to \mathbb{R}$ be uniformly continuous on $A$. \begin{enumerate}[label=(\alph*)] \item The product $x \mapsto f(x)g(x)$ is uniformly continuous on $A$. \item If there exists $k>0$ such that $|g(x)| \ge k$ for all $x \in A$, then the quotient $x \mapsto f(x)/g(x)$ is uniformly continuous on $A$. \end{enumerate}

errors.message: Extracted theorem/definition box appears to contain more than one primary statement. Extraction was preserved, but the source should be split into separate boxes and re-extracted.
errors.excerpt: Let $I \subseteq \mathbb{R}$ be an interval, let $f : I \to \mathbb{R}$, and let $c \in I$. The following are equivalent: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item $f$ is differentiable at $c$. \item There exists a function $\phi : I \to \mathbb{R}$, continuous at $c$, such that \[ f(x) = f(c) + (x-c)\,\phi(x) \qquad \text{for all } x \in I. \] \end{enumerate} In this case, $\phi(c) = f'(c)$.

errors.title: Derivative: $h$-Form Equivalence
errors.message: Extracted theorem/definition box appears to contain more than one primary statement. Extraction was preserved, but the source should be split into separate boxes and re-extracted.
errors.excerpt: Let $I \subseteq \mathbb{R}$ be an open interval, let $f : I \to \mathbb{R}$, and let $c \in I$. The following are equivalent: \begin{enumerate} \item $f$ is differentiable at $c$. \item The limit $\displaystyle\lim_{h \to 0} \frac{f(c + h) - f(c)}{h}$ exists. \end{enumerate} In this case, $\displaystyle f'(c) = \lim_{h \to 0} \frac{f(c + h) - f(c)}{h}$.
